$comment: Committed deterministic snapshot of eval/baseline-selector.mjs's exact output against the live 24-tool registry + eval-tasks.json v2 (11 tasks), captured 2026-07-20 after the Tools-v2 Option B scaffold added novada_scrape_amazon (T11 + registry + collision baseline). Tier A (bag-of-words) is FULLY DETERMINISTIC — no key, no network, no model sampling — so this snapshot is exact and safe to gate against byte-for-byte. baseline-selector.mjs gates as REGRESSION-VS-THIS-BASELINE (FIX 2, gate-hardening pass): fail if exact_accuracy drops below the value below, OR a NEW forbidden hit appears (forbidden_hits grows beyond the set below), OR a task listed here with correct:true now scores correct:false. Do NOT gate on forbidden_hits.length===0 — the 1 recorded forbidden hit (T03) is expected weak-selector noise (a bag-of-words heuristic confusing novada_search with novada_research), not a defect to chase to zero; see baseline-selector.mjs's BASELINE_FLOOR doc and eval-tasks.json's own task notes. Update this file deliberately (new date + note why) whenever eval-tasks.json or the registry's tool descriptions change on purpose — never regenerate it just to make a red run go green. 2026-07-20 UPDATE: adding novada_scrape_amazon (name/desc tokens 'amazon'/'scrape'/'product'/'listings') changed T04's Tier-A pick from novada_search (forbidden) to novada_scrape_amazon (not forbidden, still not the expected novada_scrape) — net effect: T04 stays correct:false (no true->false regression) and drops OUT of forbidden_hits (an improvement, not a new hit); T11 (new, Amazon+ASIN prompt) is a clean correct:true pick for the new tool. Exact accuracy moved 50%->55% (5/10->6/11). Verified via `npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`. 2026-07-20 UPDATE 2 (Amazon-scaffold hardening, FIX 2): T04's acceptable_tools/sibling_watch were widened to include novada_scrape_amazon (it is now an equally-correct pick for that Amazon-specific prompt, not just novada_scrape). Regenerated via the same documented command and confirmed the delta HONESTLY: this is a ZERO-delta regen for every field gated/recorded below — exact_accuracy, forbidden_hits, and every per_task.correct value (including T04's, still false) are byte-identical to the prior snapshot. That is expected, not a bug in the regen: baseline-selector.mjs's `correct` and `forbidden_hit` computations are strict equality against expected_tool / membership in unacceptable_tools — neither reads acceptable_tools at all, so widening acceptable_tools cannot move any field this file records or evaluateRegressionGate checks. The only thing that changed under the hood is the non-persisted `acceptable_accuracy` metric (T04 now scores acceptable:true instead of false, since novada_scrape_amazon is what Tier-A already picks for it) — that metric is reported by baseline-selector.mjs's live run but was never part of this committed snapshot's schema, so no field below moves. Re-verified via `npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`. 2026-07-20 UPDATE 3 (Tools-v2 search-engine platform-scraper pass — novada_scrape_google/bing/duckduckgo/yandex): registry grew 24->28 tools; eval-tasks.json grew 11->15 tasks (T12-T15, one per new platform tool, each an explicit 'raw SERP, not a synthesized answer' prompt whose expected_tool is the new per-platform tool — see each task's own notes). Regenerated via the documented command (`npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`) and recorded the HONEST delta below, including a real regression the crude Tier-A bag-of-words scorer cannot avoid: T12 and T13 both score correct:false and are NEW forbidden hits (Tier A's argmax picks novada_search for both, since its description shares more raw tokens with these prompts than the new platform tools' longer, more specific descriptions do — exactly the same class of noise already documented for T03's novada_search/novada_research confusion, now repeated for novada_search/novada_scrape_google and novada_search/novada_scrape_bing). T14 also scores correct:false (Tier A picks novada_proxy_account_list — an unrelated false-positive from shared generic tokens, not a forbidden hit). T15 scores correct:true. No previously-passing task flipped to failing (T01/T04-T11's per-task correctness is unchanged from UPDATE 2). Net effect: exact_accuracy moved 55%->46.67% (6/11->7/15) and forbidden_hits grew from {T03} to {T03, T12, T13} — both are honest, expected consequences of Tier A being a crude regression sentinel (not a quality bar, see baseline-selector.mjs's own header) confronted with harder, more specific new prompts; the model-in-the-loop Tier B runner (eval/model-eval-runner.mjs) is the real correctness signal for these tasks, not gated by this file. BASELINE_FLOOR was updated to match (both copies: eval/baseline-selector.mjs and tests/eval/baseline-selector.test.ts) — see those files' own dated comments. 2026-07-20 UPDATE 4 (Tools-v2 SOCIAL/VIDEO platform-scraper pass — novada_scrape_youtube/instagram/facebook/tiktok/x): registry grew 28->33 tools; eval-tasks.json grew 15->20 tasks (T16-T20, one per new platform tool, each a named-platform + structured-fields prompt whose expected_tool is the new per-platform tool — see each task's own notes). Regenerated via the documented command (`npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`) and recorded an UNAMBIGUOUSLY GOOD delta this time (unlike UPDATE 3): all 5 new tasks (T16-T20) score correct:true — each new platform's name/operation tokens (youtube/transcript, instagram/posts, facebook/event, tiktok/video, twitter/x/profile) are distinctive enough that Tier A's bag-of-words argmax picks the right tool cleanly, with no new forbidden hits. No previously-passing task (T01-T15) flipped to failing — every per_task.correct value below for T01-T15 is byte-identical to UPDATE 3's snapshot. Net effect: exact_accuracy moved 46.67%->60% (7/15->12/20); forbidden_hits unchanged at {T03, T12, T13}. BASELINE_FLOOR was updated to match (both copies: eval/baseline-selector.mjs and tests/eval/baseline-selector.test.ts) — see those files' own dated comments.
